Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Decision trees – the unreasonable power of nested decision rules 510 upvotes, 77 comments
  2. Computer-generated dream world: Virtual reality for a 286 processor 119 upvotes, 16 comments
  3. Neocaml – Rubocop Creator's New OCaml Mode for Emacs 40 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. Show HN: Timber – Ollama for classical ML models, 336x faster than Python 145 upvotes, 27 comments
  5. Right-sizes LLM models to your system's RAM, CPU, and GPU 188 upvotes, 39 comments
  6. Mondrian Entered the Public Domain. The Estate Disagrees 77 upvotes, 13 comments
  7. Everett shuts down Flock camera network after judge rules footage public record 303 upvotes, 86 comments
  8. An interactive intro to Elliptic Curve Cryptography 55 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS Foundation 880 upvotes, 321 comments
  10. Microsoft bans the word "Microslop" on its Discord, then locks the server 188 upvotes, 60 comments
  11. Pluralism and the Modern Poet 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Ghostty – Terminal Emulator 777 upvotes, 325 comments
  13. Go-Native Durable Execution 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Claude Code LSP 51 upvotes, 15 comments
  15. Show HN: Omni – Open-source workplace search and chat, built on Postgres 68 upvotes, 22 comments
  16. Making Video Games in 2025 (without an engine) 209 upvotes, 87 comments
  17. Show HN: Web Audio Studio – A Visual Debugger for Web Audio API Graphs 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. How to record and retrieve anything you've ever had to look up twice 87 upvotes, 32 comments
  19. Jolla phone – a full-stack European alternative 167 upvotes, 70 comments
  20. Tove Jansson's criticized illustrations of The Hobbit (2023) 191 upvotes, 95 comments
  21. /e/OS is a complete "deGoogled", mobile ecosystem 263 upvotes, 140 comments
  22. Enable CORS for Your Blog 62 upvotes, 26 comments
  23. Little Free Library 136 upvotes, 68 comments
  24. WebMCP is available for early preview 318 upvotes, 178 comments
  25. U.S. science agency moves to restrict foreign scientists from its labs 84 upvotes, 46 comments
  26. Process-Based Concurrency: Why Beam and OTP Keep Being Right 56 upvotes, 29 comments
  27. Why does C have the best file API 133 upvotes, 104 comments
  28. If AI writes code, should the session be part of the commit? 317 upvotes, 297 comments
  29. Evolving descriptive text of mental content from human brain activity 28 upvotes, 23 comments
  30. How to talk to anyone and why you should 153 upvotes, 338 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 2 March 2026. Source here.
